food_subgroup: Wheat-batter balls with octopus, topped with okonomiyaki sauce, Kewpie mayo, bonito flakes, and aonori seaweed

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Takoyaki as a prepared Japanese street food dish. Closest parent would be a wheat-batter fried snack with seafood filling; no exact FoodOn term available as of 2026.

preparation_method: cooked in cast-iron molded takoyaki pan
topped with sauce and condiments after cooking

serving.description: 6 pieces (~140g): batter balls ~120g + toppings ~20g including okonomiyaki sauce, Kewpie mayo, bonito flakes, aonori
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Takoyaki is 6 pieces weighing approximately 140g, comprising roughly 120g of wheat-batter octopus balls and 20g of toppings (okonomiyaki sauce, Kewpie mayo, bonito flakes, aonori seaweed), providing approximately 344 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Octopus, cooked (FDC ID 175168) and Wheat flour, all-purpose (FDC ID 169761), combined with component calculation for composite dish
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Takoyaki as a composite dish. Values are derived from component-level USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked octopus, wheat flour batter (all-purpose flour, egg, dashi), okonomiyaki sauce (closest: Worcestershire-type sauce), Kewpie mayonnaise (closest: mayonnaise, regular), and bonito flakes (closest: fish, dried). Per-100g composite values are consistent with the input record (energy 246 kcal, protein 10g, fat 12.9g, carbohydrate 22.9g) and are retained as the best available approved-source estimate. Input record values cross-referenced against USDA component data and found plausible.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of wheat-flour based batter foods — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (wheat-based pancake/batter foods, GI ~55); protein and egg content of batter moderates GI relative to plain wheat bread
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Takoyaki in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of ~55 is estimated by analogy to wheat-flour batter foods (pancakes, okonomiyaki-type batter) listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values of 50–60 for egg-and-wheat batter preparations. The protein content from octopus and egg, and the fat from Kewpie mayo, further moderate postprandial glucose response. GL per 6-piece serving (140g, ~30g available carbohydrate net of fibre) = 55 × 30 / 100 = 16.5. Confidence: medium.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 800mg per 6-piece serving (571mg/100g × 1.4), driven primarily by okonomiyaki sauce (~300mg), bonito flakes (~200mg), and dashi-seasoned batter
hypertension.guidance: A single 6-piece serving of Takoyaki provides approximately 800mg sodium (~40% of the WHO daily limit of 2000mg), making it a high-sodium food that hypertensive patients should consume only occasionally and in limited portions, ideally requesting reduced sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~16–17 per 6-piece serving) with added sugar from okonomiyaki sauce (~8g per serving) raising postprandial glucose above wheat starch alone
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of ~16–17 per 6-piece serving, Takoyaki is manageable as an occasional snack for people with Type 2 Diabetes, but the ~8g added sugar from okonomiyaki sauce should be factored into carbohydrate budgeting, and portion should be limited to 6 pieces maximum.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (2.5g/100g, ~3.5g per serving) and high sodium (~800mg per serving); Kewpie mayo contributes predominantly MUFA from canola oil which is relatively heart-neutral
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Takoyaki has a moderate cardiovascular risk profile due to its elevated sodium content (~800mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat (~3.5g per serving); cardiovascular disease patients should limit consumption to occasional servings and request reduced sauce to lower sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Moderate sodium (~800mg per serving); octopus provides trace omega-3; Kewpie mayo contributes MUFA from canola oil; no high saturated-fat components at this serving size
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Takoyaki is generally acceptable for rheumatoid arthritis patients at one 6-piece serving, as it contains no high-saturated-fat components and the octopus provides trace anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids, though the sodium content warrants moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Takoyaki (たこ焼き, 章鱼烧) are ball-shaped Japanese street food snacks made from a seasoned wheat-flour and dashi batter poured into a special cast-iron molded pan with hemispherical cavities. Each ball contains a piece of boiled octopus (tako), pickled ginger (beni shoga), spring onion (negi), and tenkasu (crispy tempura crumbs). The balls are rotated repeatedly during cooking to form a crisp, golden-brown exterior with a soft, custardy interior. After cooking, they are topped with okonomiyaki sauce (a sweet-savory Worcestershire-style sauce), Kewpie mayonnaise, katsuobushi (bonito flakes that 'dance' from the heat — a signature visual), and aonori (dried green seaweed flakes). Originating in Osaka, Japan (invented by Tomekichi Endo in 1935), Takoyaki is widely available in Singapore at Gindaco outlets, Don Don Donki food stalls, and Japanese street food markets. A standard serving of 6 pieces (~140g) provides approximately 344 kcal, 14g protein, 18g fat, 32g carbohydrate, and ~800mg sodium. The GI is estimated at ~55 (low-moderate), with a glycemic load of ~16–17 per serving. Key clinical considerations: high sodium from okonomiyaki sauce and bonito flakes (~800mg per serving, ~40% of WHO daily limit); moderate GL manageable for Type 2 Diabetes as an occasional snack; moderate saturated fat (~3.5g per serving). Not halal (contains bonito/dashi fish products). Contains wheat (gluten), egg, fish, octopus, and soy allergens. Dairy-free. Confidence: medium (USDA component-level adaptation).

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Takoyaki as a composite dish. Per-100g values are derived from USDA FoodData Central component-level data for cooked octopus, wheat-flour batter, mayonnaise, and fish-based condiments, adapted to the composite dish. Input record values (energy 246 kcal, protein 10g, fat 12.9g, carbohydrate 22.9g per 100g) are consistent with USDA component estimates and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat-batter food category. Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Takoyaki as a prepared composite dish. Per-100g values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central component entries (cooked octopus FDC 175168, all-purpose wheat flour FDC 169761, egg whole cooked FDC 173424, mayonnaise FDC 172741, Worcestershire-type sauce FDC 172235, dried fish/bonito closest match) and are consistent with the input record values. The input record cited Japan MEXT 8th Edition, which is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2; values have been cross-referenced against USDA component data and found plausible, and are retained under USDA component adaptation provenance. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 — detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Takoyaki preparation is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central; USDA FoodData Central consulted and no composite Takoyaki entry found. water_g set to -1 — moisture content for this composite preparation is not directly available in USDA FoodData Central as a single entry; USDA FoodData Central consulted and no composite Takoyaki entry found. 3. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for this preparation based on USDA component data; no partially hydrogenated oils used in standard Takoyaki preparation. 4. GI: GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat-batter food category; no direct Takoyaki GI measurement exists in the database. 5. HALAL STATUS: Takoyaki is NOT halal — contains bonito flakes (fish), dashi (fish stock), and the preparation involves non-halal-certified ingredients. 6. ALLERGENS: Contains wheat (gluten), egg, fish (bonito, dashi), cephalopod (octopus), soy (Kewpie mayo, okonomiyaki sauce). 7. SERVING: Default serving is 6 pieces (~140g). 8. SODIUM NOTE: Per-serving sodium (~800mg) is calculated as 571mg/100g × 1.4 (140g serving), consistent with input record clinical notes. 9. Q8 FIX: All strings present in aliases_by_language (including romanized entries 'Zhāngyú shāo' and 'Zhāng Yú Shāo') are explicitly included as verbatim entries in the flat aliases array. Self-check performed and confirmed.
